channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: What’s stopping you from buying BTC? — 147 replies — 265.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
What’s stopping you from buying BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 147 • Updated: 2026-05-31 04:21 UTC

OP

What is it? Fear? Lack of conviction? Waiting for lower prices? Or do you genuinely think Bitcoin fails long term?

Curious what’s holding people back at this stage.

Early replies (first 3)

62271721
>>62271712
Btc needs a catalyst. It probably will be yield curve control with a new name. It could also be codifying the SBR into law which is less likely. Or my personal favorite schizo theory, it's discovered that Iran was mining and selling bitcoin to fund it's endeavors and that their supply is finally exhausted.

62271922
>>62271712
Gays in it.

62271933
>>62271712
everybody and their femboy lovers above two-digits IQ are waiting for BTC to dump below at least 40k by July. Why anybody buy now? 4 years cycles never lie

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62286335
>>62271712
No money, no job
They literally won't even take me at minimum wage places because I don't speak spanish.
So, no way to generate the income to buy BTC in the first place.

62287162
>>62274011
Work for it anon

62287177
>>62283952
You can already by McDonalds with corn. Use credit card and pay off credit card with corn futures. The problem is your dumbass is bleeding wealth by keeping your net worth in corn (or companies, or anything besides bitcoin) because people are going to make more corn (and ever faster each year due to tech advances) and inflate away your wealth.

62287383
>>62285578
but why remains the hack

62287392
>>62286335
If you're married at don't have money, you deserve a divorce

62287393
>>62271712
It's a gamble and I only bet on sure things. In fact I like to have my profits guaranteed under contract and insured BEFORE I spend a dime of my own money. I always sell BEFORE I buy so I know exactly how much I'm making in advance.

62287464
Most of the retards have been bled dry. BTC only exists now as a system for whales to lead exit pumps and bleed the occasional new entrants.

62287628
>>62287383
Most of the biggest DeFi disasters over the years involved bridges, custodians, or avoidable trust assumptions somewhere in the stack. That's exactly why security architecture gets attention now.

62287630
>>62280441
You're thinking the wrong way

62287632
>>62282426
Well, depends on them

62287634
BTC has just tracked the nasdaq for years until recently and now it just sucks ass no matter what happens.

Hype has peaked. Most of it is owned by boomers now and its not a hedge against anything it just dumps the hardest on bad news until boomers, banks and third worlders get tired of bagholding and it goes sub 20k forever

62287690
>>62287634
So you're saying btc is only a big hell hype?

62288099
>>62287634
nonsense

62288112
>>62273994
So it's not worth it?

62288281
>>62271712
Indifference. I got into the market when the BTC was $3K. but I bought Link instead. Pleased with my decision. I respect what it has done and continues to do with the world but I get excited for smart contracts more, which the BTC doesn't have.

62288653
>>62271712
Because I got caught with my pants down avgcost 95k, and I've been trimming it and using that money as play dump money. So far the 15k I sold from Btc and put into Sandisk have turned in 110k already, and the 10k into gold are at 14k, and it's an actual asset. Never buying that retarded shit again. I still have like 30k and maybe I'll keep that, but chances are I'll probably sell and ride the SpaceX pump and dumo with it. Put in 50k into it, sell 50k when it doubles in price, keep the rest as gains. Did the same shit with Micron, Sandisk and silver, take profits and just keep the rest. This is a hype based market and btc has no hype anymore.

62290061
>>62288112
We'll find out when TBVs actually launch.
Then you can judge the product based on what it does, not on what random people online tell you to think.

62290076
>>62275451
Ok, just buy altcoins

62290078
>>62272002
ahahahahahahahah

62290085
>>62275501
If BTC goes to zero, alts are probably even more cooked.

62290480
>>62287464
If it's such an obvious scam, why are you still here watching it?

62290856
>>62271933
There's truth to this, but I dunno about 40k. Buy "dips" now I say

62290931
>>62272803
>>62272874

The conundrum that niggers on here don't understand is that everyone wants upside volatility, but it's the volatility itself that ultimately creates the ceiling.

It's the cute naive chick that ceases to be so once you fuck her. You can't have your cute and fuck it too.

62291823
>>62290931
You're also another nigger

62292614
>>62287393
So how much are you making?

62292863
>>62271712
the absolute best case scenario for bitcoin would be a 3x. there are other coins currently on the market that can and probably will give me a higher roi.

62292885
>>62271712
Someday a scalable bitcoin will fork away from BTC with enough ideological support to effectively upgrade what everyone will agree to be bitcoin. Then I will buy bitcoin. Until then it's pointless to buy a coin just to get the real bitcoin eventually as a dividend fork. Just wait and buy scalable bitcoin when it exists.

62292989
>>62271712
>saylor holding 5% (more due to lost coins)
>trump stink
>american hegemony stink
>quantum fud
>couldnt even act as a hedge during a war which is its only job
>2028 gary gensler revenge tour
Wasn't the plan this whole time to dump on the institutions? The OGs dumped billions and billions of market clips on them and left forever. That was the entire goal and it was accomplished, genuinely wtf more do you want? The win-con was fulfilled, if you didn't make it then come up with a new one or find another asset. Or you can continue DCAing for the next 30 years I guess

62292997
What are you getting, a 2-4x depending on where the bottom is?

62294119
>>62292863
And what is it? Low cap alts?

item.title: Honestly owning rentals made me realize how helpless alot of adults are now. — 62 replies — 248.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Honestly owning rentals made me realize how helpless alot of adults are now.

Open thread • Replies: 62 • Updated: 2026-05-31 04:53 UTC

OP

Honestly owning rentals made me realize how helpless alot of adults are now.

I bought a bunch of duplexes down south back around 2018-2020 because the numbers made sense. Cheap property taxes, cheap insurance, cheap properties in general. One duplex I paid $124k for is bringing in around $1925/mo total now and the tennants renewed instantly after the last increase.

Had a tennant last week who submitted a maintenence request because the microwave “stopped heating.” Property manager drove over there and the door wasnt shut all the way.

Another guy called because his screen door came off the track. PM slid it back in place in about 10 seconds and the guy goes “much obliged sir” like he just repaired farm equipment from the 1800s or something.

Whats weird is the older tennants almost never bother anybody. Pay on time. Maybe ask for a sink fix every few years. Younger ones send paragraphs over tiny stuff.

Not even trying to sound mean but I geniunely think people are less capable now than they were 20 years ago.

People online act like owning rentals is some impossible genius level thing but honestly half the job is just dealing with grown adults who dont know why the wifi stopped working after they unplugged the router to move furniture around.

Early replies (first 3)

62283853
>>62283816
It never sounds worth the money

62283860
How much of your revenue do you devote to hookers

62283871
>>62283816
I will buy one of these when I make it from Link. Probably in a college town. I won’t care about any profits but negotiate BJ’s for rent if they are unable to pay

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62292070
>>62284771
>it was cheap
That’s why

62292199
>>62292024
What the absolute fuck are you going on about?

62292220
>>62291978
maybe. but it's an asset that is always needed. it will always have value and you'll always be able to rent it, except of course absolute shit happens like it burning down or being squatted.

>>62283816
When i took my appartment, the previous renter had : door not closing properly (main door)and couldnt be fully locked, induction plate not working, a leak in the bathroom, cold air going under the window, many other stuff. I don't know how she lived so long like that. i fixed everything myself for like 300$, even the holes under the windows wood.

renters don't care or don't dare to do anything because they feel like they could end up in some trouble. when i fixed the door i used some power tools to recalibrate it and i took the chance i could absolutely fail. also changed the locks which were 30 years old didn't want any retard to have keys to my appt

62292222
>>62291931
sometimes a microwave will be built in to the kitchen cabinetry. otherwise a microwave is something the tenant would bring. the one exception I can think of is short term rentals where it’s unrealistic for the tenant to bring one.

62292243
>>62283964
You know there are local people that are more talented than "globofix" right??
Right?

62292248
>>62292222
ANYONE that uses a microwave is a fucking total retard.
Same with a dishwasher.
>JUST PAY HUNDREDS OF DOLLARS TO POISON YOURSELF AND RUN UP YOUR ELECTRIC BILL
>everyone signs up

I'm rich by the standards of 99% of people. Own home, land, cars, business, no debt, $500,000 liquid, PPE at business in millions.
I don't have a microwave and I removed the dishwasher to put a little wine rack.
More kitchen space. Dishes just get done after you use them and then they dry on the rack and you use them again without ever putting them away.
PACK A LUNCH AND A GOOD ONE.

62292317
>>62283816
I truly have zero desire to be a landlord but I do have an odd curiosity.
I have to wonder...how would one slumlord-max? Like, how do you take advantage of section 8 kind of housing and not give a shit about fuck while still collecting money from the government through the niggas? You know they're gonna level the place so you don't give a fuck about property value...so then...where's the breakeven? How do you profit of that kind of shit?

62292327
I hate the concept of landing and dealing w/ ubermench but I want to buy houses for my kids so they can inherit that cheap tax rate and God knows what a house will cost in 30 years

62292339
>>62292317
>how would one slumlord-max?
buy a shitty run down building and rent it out to poor people with wrecked credit but a steady job (i.e., don't rent it to jobless niggers who will never pay a dime and turn it into an EPA super fund site with crack cocaine residue.) these people have so few options that they won't dare complain to local government, because it means they'll be homeless.

you can't do this with section 8 because someone from the federal government will occasionally inspect the apartment for habitability etc.

62292353
>>62283816
>Not even trying to sound mean but I geniunely think people are less capable now than they were 20 years ago.
learned helplessness

62292360
>>62292339
That almost feels kind of philanthropic, in a weird way. I mean, if they're broke niggas but have steady jobs, there is a chance they'll actually better themselves. And if you can profit off of it in the meantime, why not?

62292458
>>62292360
just take the extra step and make the place semi decent to live anon. don't be a sicko like that

62292479
Owning rentals is a horrible investment.
Famously Trump underperformed s&p 500 by billions. he would be better off financially selling his inheritance, putting it all in s&p 500 and watching anime all day.

And that was before housing became extremely overpriced. It literally can't go up 10x in inflation adjusted terms, it would put an average person out on the street. It can go up nominally with inflation, but same is true for the stock market.

Just sell this crap and buy s&p 500

there are other arguments for owning a rental - like buy a house in a different country so you have somewhere to run away to if things get really bad. but it's not what the average person is doing.

62292484
>>62292360
it depends on how jewish you want to be. i've done walkthroughs on slumlord homes. some of them have rotting wood, collapsed sub-floors, and black mold in the bathrooms, non-functional electric baseboard heat that would probably burn the place down if you used it, and so on. i saw a 3 story multifamily built in the early 1900s with a stone foundation that was crumbling. the basement was a jungle gym of screw jacks keeping the place from collapsing. several families were actively living in the building.

62292596
>>62292479
Its good to have multiple income streams and real estate often offers significant tax advantages not to mention its much easier to secure low interest loans. Of course like any other investment you need to look for low prices but high value, even today there are many markets across the US where there is good value to be had.

62292607
>>62292596
you can easily get a securities backed loan too, at low collateral rates it will be cheaper than a mortgage.
conversely, stocks are only so much better because average person defaults to real estate

62292634
>>62283816
The funny thing about landlord stories like this is that they're built around a kind of selection bias.

You don't remember the hundreds of tenants who quietly handle small problems themselves. You remember the one who submitted a maintenance ticket because the microwave door wasn't fully closed. That story sticks because it's unusual, not because it's representative.

And honestly, people tend to overestimate how much a single anecdote proves. Someone making a mistake with an appliance doesn't mean adults have suddenly become helpless. It means a person encountered something that appeared broken and reported it through the channel available to them.

The same logic could be applied anywhere. A customer asks an employee where something is instead of searching the entire store. A homeowner calls a mechanic for a noise that turns out to be minor. A business owner hires an accountant because they don't understand tax law. Most people rely on specialists for things outside their expertise. That's normal, not evidence of decline.

What's interesting is that the economics of the story tell a different tale than the conclusion. A duplex purchased for $124k generating roughly $1,925 a month in rent suggests the tenants are providing a significant return on the asset. They're not passive observers in that arrangement; they're the reason the investment performs in the first place.

So if the strongest example of widespread helplessness is someone misdiagnosing a microwave, that's actually a pretty weak case. Human beings have always overlooked obvious things. We notice those moments because they're memorable, not because they're new.

62292693
>>62292607
If I put 70k down on a home and rent it out netting $1200/mo after all expenses thats a 20% return. Thats already better than the long term average for the S&P500. If I raise the rent and am now netting $1500/mo thats 25%.

>stocks are only so much better because average person defaults to real estate
Most people who own real estate only own the house they live in. Conversely most people with a 401k, a pension or even an IRA will be in the S&P so not sure where you're getting the idea that real estate investing is the normie choice.

62292700
>>62292693
you are not getting 20% apr with mortgage payments, what is this financial fantasy
rent per month in the us is below monthly mortgage payments, and that doesn't even include maintenance
your reply tells me you have zero knowledge about the matter

62292741
>>62292693
>If I put 70k down on a home and rent it out netting $1200/mo after all expenses thats a 20% return.

$1200/month profit on a SFH is rare. most landlords are targeting 1% rule (gross monthly rent is 1% of property purchase price) with 50% of gross monthly rents going to actual and budgeted overhead.

let's say $250k purchase price with 20% down @ 6.5% for 30 years. let's pretend it will rent for $2500/month. in the cities you showed it probably will not, because people living there are poor as hell. but let's just pretend.

>$1250/mo mortgage
>$150/mo property insurance
>$300/mo property taxes
>$50/mo vacancy budget (~2.5% gross rents or 10 days a year)
>$100/mo maintenance budget (rentoids are tough on properties)

we're already up to $1850/mo in expenses on $2500/mo gross rents, so that's $650/mo profit before taxes. should we assume the rentoids are responsible for mowing the lawn and shoveling sidewalks? that might be a risk, because local laws might pin that responsibility on the landlord even if it's in the lease (i.e., your property collects fines and/or liens for violations.)

62292883
>>62292741
he's just a larper
larpers need a chatgpt plugin to tell them if what they are posting is too retarded to be believable

62292892
>>62292741
>$1200/month profit on a SFH is rare.
Well thats literally what I'm getting on my duplex. I put 140,000 down on a 700,000 duplex my costs all in are just under $3600/mo. Lets add $300/mo for maintenance/vacancy just to make you happy. That still gives me a ~18% return at current rent but the rent will always increase while my mortgage stays fixed. And when its payed off I can either pocket that extra money or take out another loan if rates are low and use it for further investments.
And yes they mow their own fucking lawn its in the rental agreement otherwise I send someone to do it and charge them whatever it costs + an administrative penalty.

BTW I'm not disagreeing that its good to invest in the stock market that is how I make most of my money, all I'm saying is that its good to have multiple diverse sources of income so that your finances are secure no matter what. If you're in your 20's with only a few thousand dollars then yes just keep working and invest as much as you can in high growth stocks to maximize your return but once you get closer to 7 digits you need to start thinking about wealth preservation and tax efficiency not just pure growth.

62293005
>>62292892
>my costs all in are just under $3600/mo
a $560k mortgage at 6.5% for 30 years is $3.5k a month. you therefore bought before COVID, which means this discussion is completely pointless -- because time travel does not exist.

62293042
>>62292741
Borrowing to lease should be illegal. Solves all problems.

62294103
>>62292199
Profit in europe for renting out real estate is between 3% to 5% of the purchasing value, at least in my country. Extorting niggers is illegal here.

62294126
>>62292248
Fucking based. Goywave and goywasher users btfod.

62294140
>>62292596
Lol, every one of those cities is full of niggers. I wonder why nobody wants to buy houses there.

62294158
>>62292634
stop posting AI slop. >>62294132 was enough

62294161
>>62292248
HELL YEH THESE YOUNGINS OF TODAY DON'T KNOW HOW GOOD THEY HAVE IT

MEEMAW DID EVERYTHING ON THE WOOD STOVE BACK WHEN I WAS A BOY WE ALL TOOK TURNS WASHING THE DISHES

=================================
BILL STONEHOUSE
USMC 1968-1969
GRADUATED SCHOOL OF HARD KNOCKS
pHD FROM UNIVERSITY OF LIFE

62294172
>>62283816
>Whats weird is the older tennants almost never bother anybody. Pay on time. Maybe ask for a sink fix every few years. Younger ones send paragraphs over tiny stuff.
Maybe because the younger ones know their rights, while boomers were raised to tip their landlords and billionaires?
It’s YOUR property, asshole. You own it, you fix it Mister Moneybags, what the fuck are you doing with the money anyway? I’m not obliged to lift a fucking finger owning or maintaining something that’s not mine, especially if I’m shilling out nearly two large a month for it.
I hate negligent landlords, I’ve dealt with too many assholes like you that refuse to deal with broken appliances or tenant issues while you sit back and collect a check.

item.title: Kek XLM is absolutely ripping and is up multiple places overtaking monero and link while stinkies are at $8.99 LMAO HAHA… — 49 replies — 196.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Kek XLM is absolutely ripping and is up multiple places overtaking monero and link while stinkies are at $8.99 LMAO HAHA…

Open thread • Replies: 49 • Updated: 2026-05-31 04:54 UTC

OP

Kek XLM is absolutely ripping and is up multiple places overtaking monero and link while stinkies are at $8.99

LMAO HAHAHAHAHAHA OH NO NO LINKIES OH NO NO

Early replies (first 3)

62287876
>>62287844
we aren't cuckolds, okay? We just like knowing that Sergey keeps getting richer while Chainlink is stuck at 2020 prices

62288485
kek stinkies

62288499
>>62287844
xlm always does this before the bull..

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62289745
>>62289658
claiming that "they're going to use all kinds of chains" inherently implies that they're going to use the corresponding blockchain tokens as well, since you know, that's required when interacting with each chain
having said that, please point to me one instance of DTCC stating that they're using the LINK token

62289756
>>62289745
>please point to me one instance of DTCC stating that they're using the LINK token
The Chainlink products they're using need the Link token.

62289763
>>62289756
>can't even bring up a single instance

62289813
>>62289763
Instance of what?

62289925
>>62287844
I don't care about your (((compliant))) coin

62290228
>>62287844
I started the link cuckold meme. KNEEL

62290247
>>62289745
Chainlink will literally abstract away any gas token

One token all chains (the one and only LINK)

62290559
>>62287844
MOM IT'S DOING IT AGAIN

62290596
>>62289745
kek see this >>62290247

Not only are they going to be buying Link tokens, but they'll be buying Link tokens in order to pay for the gas on the corresponding blockchain. Because the fee in Link takes care of the gas.

62290754
>>62288539
I’ve fucked two guys wives before and filmed it

Idk what possesses dudes to be into this kind of shit but I got paid so

62290965
Xlm will actually hit $1

62291397
>xlm +43%
Damn linkies are fucking cooked.. when's the last time link had a pump like that? 2019??

62291419
>xlm up 100%
Kek linkies

62291508
>Almost in the top #10

Kek stinkies wish they had this kind of performance

62291680
KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOm

62291689
>>62287844
Wow XLM sure is running! It must be so much higher than it was a year ago, right?????

62291699
>>62291680
so bullish chainlink

>>62291689
KABOOOOOM!

62291706
>>62291508
>pay tens of millions to Chainlink per year so you can be part of SCALE
>DTCC announces they will be using Chainlink to bridge tokenized assets to XLM using CCIP and exclusively using CRE for orchestration

>LINK -9%
>XLM +80%

62291709
>>62291706
Say it with me

The ... of crypto

62291787
>>62289658
>yet this is how the market reacts.
>No wonder crypto is getting is ass beat by the stock market so badly.

62291789
>>62291787
>market is never wrong

Thats why nothing has ever changed prices in history of human kind

62291989
>>62291787
Stocks are high in the sky and crypto is in the crapper, despite mass institutional adoption finally starting.

Stock market = right
Crypto market = wrong

62292014
>>62291709
THE cuckolds of crypto

62292427
>>62288957
> Big wallets keep accumulating and stacklets keep selling

Can you give a credible source that backs up your claims of whales accumulating and small wallets capitulating

62292431
KABOOOOOOOOOOM

62292813
>>62291989
>>62291789
My nw is 90% in crypto.
The market valuation of chainlink however, is 100% correct.

62293352
Xlm bros…
We got too cocky

62293596
>>62290228
Extremely based

62293668
>>62289756

More demonstration of muh price feeds meaning nothing to token holders. You were always wrong.

62294176

item.title: The Market Turbo Shits, But I Rise Like Jeet Flies — 63 replies — 47.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
The Market Turbo Shits, But I Rise Like Jeet Flies

Open thread • Replies: 63 • Updated: 2026-05-31 03:35 UTC

OP

BAT bros? You still there? What the fuck is going on? LINK shits. BTC dumps deadly diarrhea. Orange man shitposts. But this piece of shit is +15%? Is Lobes flipping the switch? Hold me. Also. Jennie lewds please

Early replies (first 3)

62285628
no one still owns BAT
... right?

62285632
>>62285628
Apparently I do. Jennie doesn't though

62285713
YOU KNOW THE DEAL.

TP - WHERE ARE MY THOTS AT? IT'S BEEN A WHILE.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62290540
>>62290344
>I'm getting like 5GB+ memory usage from a single YouTube tab]
its a known bug yt's end, the like and share (etc) interface below the video is caught in an endless live loop
to fix it do this: go to settings then find shields then select content filtering then scroll down to create custom filters and paste this in the box field www.youtube.com###menu
then save changes
let me know how you get on

62290561
>>62290344
>I'll switch PCs in a bit and see if I can find the profile for you.
thanks TP, you're the best of 'em
>Hey Lad! Long time, no see
I'm working abroad again
trying to get settled in
what about you? doing well I hope

62290662
>>62290540
I really appreciate that, lad! Thanks a lot! I'll give it a go in a bit.

>>62290561
>you're the best of 'em
Back at you!
>I'm working abroad again
Where do they have you this time?

62290702
>>62290662
yw TP, that fix is a life saver, but if you're still having issues then tell me as I have a few more filters that will help
back in kong of the hong variety for as long as needed
hot damn that is one holy soft thicc and wobbly thot

62290728
>>62290344
this bitch is just ridiculous

62290732
>>62290662
HOLY FUCK LORD HAVE MERCY

62290812
>>62290702
Seems to be doing a lot better. I really appreciate it!
>HK
sick! I might be going to Shanghai sometime later in the Summer. If I make it out there, I'll let you know. Hope you have a comfy stay!

>>62290728
>>62290732
I got the name for you >>62286122
Cece Rose. I'll dump more of the OF if you don't want to pay for it.

_
Might get a vacay for this one, but fuck it.

62290849
>>62290812
cool TP
yeah let me know
this apartment is way better than the last place I was assigned thank fuck
kekeke we'll see you next thread ig but worth it lel
jannies might have chilled tho I hope

62290910
>>62290812
LEGEND!

I'm gonna need the name of this goth whore next. Are those braces?

62291155
>>62290355
>>62290662
>>62290812
Fucking hell, TP. More of this please, especially >>62290355

62291188
>>62285628
Nope. Sold everything when it became clear they’re not actually interested in building utility for the token. It’s a memecoin connected to a browser. They had the world by the balls and they made a conscious decision to do absolutely nothing for BAT. They cannot be trusted

62291222
>>62290355
You're a pornographer, the fuck you care?

62291254
>>62286450
>>62290355
>>62290662
>>62290812
TP MOGS /gif/
THE MOTHERFUCKING GOAT

62291656
>>62290812
Wonderful rack on her. Lets see some asian MILFS GILFS

62291660
>>62287337
she's hands down the hottest crypto girl, the hottest thing about her is that she hides how big her ass is, won't even show it off out of modesty

62291667
>>62289174
Most BAT holders moved on to something else eventually, but still hold some BAT out of loyalty and the purest copium you'll ever find

Anyone who was "all-in" on BAT has sold or killed themselves by now

62292238
>>62286450
need more of this one lads

62292673
>>62291660
i want to see her tiddies so bad

62292929
>>62290849
>this apartment is way better than the last place I was assigned
I'm happy for you ^_^!

>>62290910
>Are those braces?
Yes sir!

>>62291155
Czech'd and I should be around to post more later tonight for you.

>>62291254
That means more than you know, lad! Thanks for the love <3

>>62291656
>Lets see some asian MILFS GILFS
Short on time rn, but if you want something better than this, lmk and I'll see what I can do later.

62293156
>>62292929
>Czech'd and I should be around to post more later tonight for you.

oh baby. Trans jans are letting TP poast freely for once in a blue moon, love to see it

62293250

item.title: crypto shills destroyed /biz/ — 20 replies — 9.9/hr

crypto shills destroyed /biz/

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-05-31 02:53 UTC

OP

you stupid shitcunts nobody is going to buy your fucken dumb digital coin that has no fucken use case

You were too late for bitcoin

you got psychologically destroyed by millennial and gen x goobers posting on twitter from their rented condos in miami

And now you're here trying to tiktok dance your way into fucken millions

GTFO

Early replies (first 3)

62293753
>>62293749
don't you have some dying boomer business to take care of lol

62293756
>>62293753
wowee

*does the fortnite dance*

feed my more low i.q. shortform slop sir!

Imagine being a GEN Z male who cannot even focus long enough to fill out a fucken business plan

62293763
>>62293756
imagine being so small brained that you have to work with dirty pipes since its the only way you wont lose everything. its indeed better you leave the crypto to smarter people and focus on the piss and shit

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62293780
>>62293763
>imagine being so small brained that you have to work with dirty pipes since its the only way you wont lose everything. its indeed better you leave the crypto to smarter people and focus on the piss and shit


You are absolutely going to get everything you deserve.

62293782
>>62293749
>crypto shills destroyed /biz/
/biz/ was literally made for crypto shills to destroy

62293792
>>62293749
>you stupid shitcunts
>no fucken use case
yeh nah get fucked bruce my coins are heaps good. and the use case is making me shekels ya pseud poof
>>62293756
>business plan
cope from normie goys too afraid to risk-on. tism is my business plan

62293809
>>62293792
net worth? liquid, not illiquid.

62293821
>>62293749
/biz/ was the crypto shill board since day 1 of its creation, lol

t. OG /biz/tard

62293822
>>62293821
>>62293821
thank you. this is what i wanted to hear.

62293826
>>62293809
i'll put it this way: i've roundtripped low 7figs and it only mildly annoyed me.

funny that you throw away your concerns about "use case" and go straight to money talk. that's what it's always about in the end

62293831
>>62293780
pokemon cards are also trending. no one sane does hard labor anymore, pays like shit and breaks your body and you actually have to go to physical locations

62293845
>>62293826
>Anyone disciplined enough to work 18 hour days, can, save money, get a degree, become an actual asset, start a business, reinvest their earnings and reach eight figures before 50.

exactly you fucken retards rely on other retards to buy your crypto

you have no fucken business

you have no actual talent

you're just a fucken shitlord that relies on retards

but that is ok

because the money you make can pay for status and can pay for objects to fill the void!

62293850
>>62293831
thanks, i can see the trajectory there.

62293875
>>62293845
how can you be a business person and not see why crypto would have value? it doesn't have to make sense, if there is demand for it then it has value. people out there are paying to see pictures of a girls boobs, you can monetize literally anything these days if you got the brains for it

62293876
>>62293845
>exactly you fucken retards rely on other retards to buy your crypto
yes, i rely on exit liquidity in markets. that's correct. wild revelation! but i also have the balls to bid heavy in the first place. which, i can tell from your seethe, you do not
>because the money you make can pay for status and can pay for objects to fill the void!
i am literally the stealth wealth meme of the guy who still gets around in a toyota corolla and wears average clothes. plus a couple cheap vintage seikos. that's it. walking past me on the street you'd never assume my nw. i never signal status, idc about any of that.

sorry u have no balls to bid heavy, fag. i bet on myself and i win. you're the sour pseudo-intellectual cunt who scoffs from the sidelines then calls them "lucky" when it works out

62293920
>>62293876
No no you're right. Like, it would have been epic if you cashed out on the low 7 figs.

And you do need courage, balls etc to take risk. You are correct.

But any miami cunt who comes at me trying to shill crypto will get crushed under my intellect.

If you're honest bout the game it's all good imo

62293926
>>62293876
also the stealth thing is pretty smart desu. lowers vectors of attack against you. status signalling does have some practical uses but you probably already are smart enough to know when and where to flaunt your status.

62293934
>>62293920
anyone who is spiritually miami deserved to be crushed, that is true.

i'm just some bloke who used tism pattern recognition and intuition to financially help himself and his family. i'm not sorry for any of it and i'd do it all again. i pay my tax like a good goy and don't have to wagie because of this, literally every day i'm free to do as i please. it was worth it desu. take risks in life. doesn't have to be crypto, fuck crypto if you hate it. just bet on yourself in some avenue of life. risk it all.

62293943
>>62293934
for sure, those are great points. And well done!

62294000
>>62293753
What's a non boomer business?

item.title: SATA bought over 2k BTC this week — 11 replies — 6.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
SATA bought over 2k BTC this week

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-05-31 03:15 UTC

OP

The new investment offering 13% in daily dividends bought 2k BTC this week.

Strive the company behind SATA only owns 15k BTC so far so this is huge.

It's marked to have another huge day today probably going to finish around another 500 BTC bought.

Early replies (first 3)

62288571
wow 2K btc

to the moon

62288604
>>62288571
They grew their BTC treasury by 10% in one week.
2k BTC is more than El Salvador bought in the last 2 years.

This is rocket fuel because everyone is getting 13% and BTC is getting slurped up.
This will send the price to 250k in a year.

62290655
Holy Bitcoin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62291854
>>62288531
>13% APR paid daily
We understand that this is unsustainable, right? This is an obscene amount to be paying out, it cannot last forever at this percentage. What's their exit strategy? Is there any?

62292377
>>62291854
It's 13% for the year, if you auto buy more with the dividend it's 13.88% the dividend pays everyday.

They're strategy is that Bitcoin goes up 30%+ a year and they make loads of money.

They're chief risk officer interviewed coffeezilla and destroyed him in a debate, it's legit.

62292430
>>62290655
So they literally just copied Saylors' Ponzi scheme but threw an extra 3% payout on top to make sure it collapses faster.

62292449
>>62288531
Oh boy oh boy, if these schemes don't collapse in the next 2 years, it might literally the catalyst of next bear market bottom.

Saylor's migrated some bitcoin to Coinbase and Nakamoto Inc. selling and reporting huge losses.

I hope these niggers in fancy suits are taking into account that btc will at highest reach 200k next bull

https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/crypto/articles/bitcoin-firm-nakamotos-stock-hits-150928904.html

62292487
>>62292377
I like Jeff Walton. I like Michael Saylor. I don't own mstr or asst or any of the preferreds. I want to see how the market reacts when a bitcoin bull market kicks off. I know they'll be able to issue the common stock as mnav goes up. My curiosity is what happens to the preferred shares? I know it's a variable rate and it will inevitably force a raise to keep it at par but will that be enough or will they have to buyback the shares as well? So now instead of using funds to increase the bitcoin exposure, they're forced to deleverage. Then to make the common stock attractive again, they either issue debt to lever up the balance sheet or sell bitcoin to buyback the common stock. It's not a ponzi but it's extremely cyclical unless bitcoin does just go up only but it still seems extremely cyclical as well. Definitely interesting but I'd rather just hold bitcoin.

62292499
>>62292449
if they can remain solvent until 2029 they won

62292640
>>62288531
Fuck that theyre run by that poojeet vivek ramaswamy. All bizraelies should buy microstrategy and support the white man. Not to mention poojeets history of scamming people with his old biotech company

62294034
>>62292449
I guess that's the problem with treasury plays. They can fucking sell it and you end up with a trash stock that doesn't even have the bitcoin you bought it for.

item.title: *continues to predict the price of BTC in your path* All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're… — 72 replies — 14.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
*continues to predict the price of BTC in your path* All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're…

Open thread • Replies: 72 • Updated: 2026-05-30 23:55 UTC

OP

*continues to predict the price of BTC in your path*

All you had to do was buy on green and sell on red, at least we're close to a buy signal now

prev thread:
>>62261213

Early replies (first 3)

62284885
How do I use this?

62284969
>>62284885
green = buy dips
red = sell rips
yellow = volatility likely, derisk
the site has a realtime FX-based confirmation layer - positive confirms buys and invalidates sells, negative confirms sells and invalidates buys
today FX is solidly green so early buys are defensible
chart gets updated every month with a new 4-week, next will be on Sunday

62284994
imagine buying bitcoin over 70K

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62285940
>>62285919
you probably work for the cia retard

62285967
>>62285940
>CIA
>giving away the free money cheat code to internet autists
doesn't seem very likely anon

62286025
>>62285967
I mean as long as this doesn't go full premium (as a ploy to ensnare gullible /biz/ tards) it seems like a good resource. Some ppl on this board want to stay poor or just FUD for fun. Pathetic behavior honestly.

62286076
>>62286025
It's nowhere close to where I'd feel comfortable charging people money for it. Trade bots/API feeds will probably be subscription, when that's done, but those are more for pro traders
There's also daily updates on the twitter account

62286347
>>62285878
>outputs start to get a little blurry 4-5 weeks out and then just turn into noise, which is why I do the updates every 4 weeks
Why not update weekly? or even daily?

62286447
>>62286347
>Why not update weekly? or even daily?
Basically, because it's a lot easier - there are a lot of inputs into the model that each have to be harvested and processed by me before they get fed into the sim. It runs into the hours each time. I can also cope that the current date's "conditions" are obviously the starting point of the forecast, so having them flattening out a little bit is easier to align
I do weekly updates on the site and the twitter account, but that's 90% just applying current price momentum and FX momentum to the existing physics-based forecast, which I have to get better at. I'm a nerd, not a trader

62286532
>>62284814
>buy may 17
>sell for a loss on May 25

thanks

62286552
>>62286532
>buy may 17
>short may 25
you're welcome

62286639
>>62286447
>because it's a lot easier
Fair enough. Although I'm sure at least some of that data gathering and processing could be automated.
>I'm a nerd, not a trader
So you don't use it all? I get that some people are very risk averse but if you're confident in your technology I don't see why you wouldn't at least dip your toes and make what is essentially free money.

I think I might do a bit of paper trading and I'll let you know how it pans out by the end of June.

62286700
>>62286639
>Fair enough. Although I'm sure at least some of that data gathering and processing could be automated.
A lot of it is! I keep getting tempted to outsource some of it to an LLM but some gut instinct says "you'd be really really dumb to do that," even a local one
>So you don't use it all? I get that some people are very risk averse but if you're confident in your technology I don't see why you wouldn't at least dip your toes and make what is essentially free money.
Oh, I use it, have been trading perps with it for years, getting more profitable as the model evolves. After the addition of the FX layer last spring, I can get away with some truly disgusting leverage. I just meant I don't have a background in trading or technical analysis, so I've been learning all of those skills on the go. It's all related but still a different skillset
>I think I might do a bit of paper trading and I'll let you know how it pans out by the end of June.
Please do, I'll post in here when there's updates but you can always get in touch via Twitter

62287275
>>62284814
I’m just gonna wait for the mega dump to flush the longs down to 60k..

62289468
>>62284814
L, and let me be perfectly clear, FG

62289671
>>62289468
So up from here? Don’t speak zoomzoom

62289838
>>62289671
This is where price should turn up - hybrid FX+physics says Buy because FX is so positive, physics officially flips green tomorrow, it all seems pretty good for bulls over the next 24 hours.

62289954
>>62287275
this. I just go all in bottom of the bear market and do nothing a couple years

62291704
>>62284814
This is no proper backtesting! You must test at least 30 instances!
https://youtu.be/zXoZdnYfIlA?si=MPB49se3bA1H2fnp

62291711
>>62291704
*32 Instances

62291719
>>62291711
>>62291704
?

62291779
>>62291719
How big are the wins and how big are the losses? They have to be in a profitable proportion together with the win rate. 66% win rate with wins 1/3 as big as the losses is not profitable for example.

62291951
>>62284814
broken clock o algo

62292012
what time of day do you make the trades

62292563
if you have a profitable strategy then just make a hyperliquid vault so we can join it and you have 10% profit share

62292730
>>62291779
There's a whole website and twitter account where you can check these things
>>62292012
>a broken clock predicts the future 80% of the time
That's one hell of a broken clock
>>62292012
The demo strategy executes ~2 hours into NY open, when there's enough FX data to confirm or inval the signal
>>62292563
Down the list of things to do. Right now it's a trade bot, which is being built because a mid-tier CEX wants to explore white labelling signals, so the bot needs to be optimized, but then I'll absolutely integrate it into HL

62292891
>>62292730
Thank you, I already have working strategies. I was just advocating doing proper backtesting before applying any strategy.

62292918
>>62292891
>I was just advocating doing proper backtesting before applying any strategy.
Always a good practice - prediction/outcome goes back to December 2024 which is where the hit rate is from. I could backtest to mid-2024 (when the FX layer was added) if I feel like it but I figured 100+ signals over more than a year was good enough

62293541
Spoonfeed me pls OP? Am I supposed to follow your twitter? How do I get the meme lines for myself.

62293559
>>62293541
>How do I get the meme lines for myself.
I'll post them in here, but there's also a live dashboard and a twitter, where a follow/likes would be greatly appreciated
>>62285878

62293571
>>62293559
Thanks for doing the needful sir. I'll be checking this out. Big ups anon.

62293619
>>62292730
Does the trading bot have contingencies in place just in case other trading bots start countertrading your strategy if it ends up being too successful?

62293680
>>62293619
I think if someone else's trading bot can move BTC by a few percent per day then we all have much bigger problems

item.title: UH OH WAGIES — 42 replies — 8.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
UH OH WAGIES

Open thread • Replies: 42 • Updated: 2026-05-31 00:08 UTC

OP

Bezos' rocket just blew up

Early replies (first 3)

62286977
>>62286974
well at least it looked cool

62286979
>>62286974
>>62286977
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62286993
buying the amazon dip tomorrow

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62287052
CLEAN UP THE DEBRIS, WAGIE!!!

62287125
>>62286974
bezos can suck the shit of my asshole

62287128
>>62286974
So? Eill stonks go down and crypto up now? No? So?

62287260
>>62286974
AMAZON x XRP PARTNERSHIP?????????

BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!

62287271
>>62287052
what an ugly freak god damn nigga even with 270 billion dollars being this ugly has got to be rough.

62287278
Gender studies be one of them classes where you realize society really out here making random rules then acting like them rules dropped from the sky . The whole subject talks about how people get treated different based on gender, race, class, and identity. It ain’t just about “men versus women.” Nah, it’s deeper than that. It’s about power, who got it, and who keeps the system running like a broken carnival ride .

Back in the day, society pushed these roles heavy. Dudes supposed to be tough, work jobs, never cry. Girls supposed to stay pretty, quiet, cook food, and smile through suffering like customer service employees on minimum wage . Gender studies basically walks into the room and says, “Yo, who invented these goofy rules?” That’s where people start talking about gender as a social construct. Meaning society made a lot of these expectations up over time and everybody just downloaded the update without reading the terms and conditions .

Another big thing in gender studies is intersectionality. Big word, simple meaning. Different struggles stack together like bad Wi-Fi connections. Somebody can face problems because of gender, race, money, or all three at once. The system don’t hit everybody equally. Some people start life on easy mode while others spawn into expert difficulty with no tutorial .

Media also catches strays in this class because movies, ads, music, and social media keep pushing stereotypes 24/7. Folks grow up seeing the same roles repeated until they think it’s “normal.” Gender studies tries to challenge those dominant narratives and make people question why society moves the way it does.

At the end of the day, the class teaches that identity, power, and culture all connected. Also teaches students how capitalism be profiting off stereotypes while everybody else just trying to survive and pay rent .

62287284
NO BATHROOM BREAKS TOMORROW

12 HOUR SHIFTS SAT AND SUN

62287347
>>62287260
so bullihs at this..

62287354
>>62287271
He literally looks like any other MF in the street

62287575
>>62286974

>didn't even leave the ground
did they forget to undo the bolts?

62287805

62287806
>>62286974

62287807
>>62287805

62287808
>>62286974
>"spacex will be overvalued!!!"

62287893

62290349
>>62286974
why is there this stupid space race? there aint shit up there

62290482
>>62290349
It's all a huge scam. None of them care about or actually intend to go to space. What they're doing is creating the framework to sell a dream to investors. By the time everyone realizes it's never going to happen, the people running the scam will have cashed out and moved onto the next scam.

62290608
>>62286974
Where did the tower go? Can rocket fuel melt steel beams?

62290641
>>62286979
KABOOOOOMMM!!! BULLISH AS FUCK FOR XRP!!!!

62290658
>>62290349
>There ain't shit up there

Precisely. Zero niggers. The white man will have finally have peace.

62290712
>>62290658
Have you seen the recent astronauts visiting “space?”

62292472
>>62287052
>Power washer? A bucket of water and a sponge is good enough.

62292498
>>62292472

62293691
>>62286974
Plot Twist: The explosion is so dramatic that the whole world decides to YOLO into rocket club driving absolute rocket mania. This hype raises Virgin Galactic from the poor house back to Blingville and Space X goes to Space Lord Tier.

62293694
>>62290482
I mean Musk has been doing SpaceX since the early 2000s. I think hes just autistic about space

62293710
>>62287805
Virgin Galactic because it got first pic on ticker symbols so it chose SPCE. It's also very cheap right now with a market cap around 600 Million and Space X is seeking up to 2 Trillion market cap under ticker symbol SPCX. That's coming in June if nothing delays it. All details aside, the word in everybody's brains is space and the hype is buy, buy, buy. The next few weeks are going to be very interesting.

item.title: I made a drink that was revealed to me in a dream. How do I sell it? — 27 replies — 6.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
I made a drink that was revealed to me in a dream. How do I sell it?

Open thread • Replies: 27 • Updated: 2026-05-31 00:58 UTC

OP

It's one of the most delicious things I've ever had. Genuinely top 10 drinks. I wanna sell it, but... How? Who do I pitch this to? How tf do you market "this recipe was revealed to me in a dream?" Everyone who's tried it loves it but IDK where to go from here.

Early replies (first 3)

62290943
>>62290941
moon brew...

62290953
>>62290941
go to pepsico
walk in the front door
find the owner
stand up straight, look him in the eye, give him a firm handshake
tell him you had a dream about a drink and pepsis and money
????
profit

62290960
give me the recipe and i'll tell you if it's actually good. i have god tier taste buds and taste in general. . Normies fear my eloquent opinions.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62290979
>>62290960
I'm absolutely not giving up the recipe. Dream your own drinks.
>>62290953
It's gotta be firm or it doesn't work

62291614
If it has to be made fresh? Form an LLC, save up enough money to rent out a small store or street facing bar/stand whatever with signage out front etc. and make it known locally. If you are truly destitute poor then set up a table at all the local farmers markets in every town within 5 hours of you and sell it there. Hire hot women to sell it at these stands/markets/stores once you've saved enough money then get it to start trending on social media short form videos hiring hot girls and guys to drink it in videos so people will travel there to try it

62291617
>>62291614
if it's any good it would take professional tasters about 3 minutes to figure out exactly how to copy it and set up competing businesses.

that's the problem with food in the US. You can't actually stop rivals from copying your recipe

62291622
>>62291617
add some natural food coloring to it to throw them off for a few more years. Doesn't need to be blue or something just make it the wrong neutral natural looking color. OP i want to taste your delicious drink

62291625
>>62291622
lol

changing the color isn't going to throw off anyone that tastes food for a living

and that just makes it easier to copy
>OP sues competitor for copying his drink
>but yours is blue, ours isn't!
>lawsuit gets tossed
>OP dies in a back alley for lack of a working business model

62291633
>>62291625
this is like saying you should never start a business because there are other businesses out there so don't try. There aren't going to be professional taste testers at the farmers market trying to set up another beverage table lmao. Even if there were, it will take years for a major business to roll out their copy to the masses going through all the legal trouble and logistics and figuring out how to get it shelf stable in bottle and OP will have already made good money off it by then just like what happens to every good new product

62291638
>>62291633
I'm talking about the point where he's renting buildings and setting up new locations

at that point mcdonalds starbucks and about 5000 other bars and restaurants are going to send tasters out and in a matter of weeks will have sourced bulk ingredients, safety and marketing tested the drinks, and implemented them across thousands of existing stores

>how to get it shelf stable in bottle
that's not how restaurants and bars in the US sell things.

62291641
>>62291638
Yeah I can see that happening but that will take time, he still has time to make money from it before then, and if he wants he still has a decent shot at marketing his street facing walk up bar as having "the original moon brew" or whatever and can still make some money. What I'm saying is that he should still try so that I can taste this thing

62291647
>>62291641
Yes, it can be done. He's probably going to have to sell to a competitor eventually though I'd guess. Or take on investors to get the capital to go big.

I'm lazy, I'd just take the recipe directly to someone that already has the marketing and locations to produce it in place right off the bat.

62291649
>>62291647
those are all good ideas and would make money from a dream which is pretty funny, i'm rooting for you OP it better not be chocolate milk with saffron added or some shit

62291651
>>62291649
>it better not be chocolate milk with saffron added or some shit
kek'd out loud

I bet it is.

>Orange juice with vanilla, milk, and an egg made billions

62291662
>>62290941
you dont unless your dream told you how to sell it.

you will fail

62291664
>>62291651
kek now i want an orange julius god this thread makes me thirsty, i hope it's something strange like that

62293191
>>62290941
you made chocolate milk that looks like barf and want to sell it?

62293358
>>62291617
you say that, but nobody has managed to figure out coke's recipe in almost a century.

62293376
>>62293358
have you ever tried store-brand colas?

62293478
>>62290941
It’s got cum in it, doesn’t it?

62293488
>>62290941
thanks for this anon, just made a drink of orange, lime juice, beet powder, sugar, carbonated raspberry water
maybe I'll sell it, top 10 fruity drink for sure

62293646
>>62290941
I have that glass.

62293674
>>62290941
What's in it as ingredients, not a combo? What will you call it so we can know how to look for it?

62293703
>>62290941
There was some fucktard on biz way back when it was a new board, I think his name was Riley when he used a trip, and he kept posting about his invention that would revolutionize kitchens around the world. Everyone would have one. He wouldn’t tell us what it did but believed very strongly in it and just wanted help patenting or licensing his invention. Well before ally spilled the beans as they say and posted a retarded MS Paint drawing of his retarded invention: a tortilla chip warmer. Like a low powered air fryer basically.
We all laughed and then he never posted again.

62293708
>>62291651
Orange whip? Orange whip?

Three orange whips.

62293779
>>62290941
>cocoa milk with banana

item.title: cardano, ADA is imploding right now — 64 replies — 6.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
cardano, ADA is imploding right now

Open thread • Replies: 64 • Updated: 2026-05-30 19:05 UTC

OP

cardano, ADA
is imploding right now

Early replies (first 3)

62275698
the whole space is fucking dying. sad that it all ended up this way

62275723
>>62275591
Charles-sama, why?

62276473
>>62275591
Imagine buying cardanus, ever! This shit has less than 500$ stables liquidity onchain.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62281159
I never trusted Charles so I never bought cardano…dude is an obvious psycho

62281608
ENJOY YOUR CARDANO, EARTHMAN!

62281639
>>62275591
>@TheCardanoTimes

62281653
>>62281159
This, one look at that basedboi faggot should tell you all you need to know

If you still made the decision to put money in this project, its your fault, really

62282581
>>62277151
fucking kek charles is such a fucking narcissist

62282732
>>62277151
Wow that's very bold and stupid. Homeboy thought he could just jump into an industry as complex as healthcare. "Mayo Clinic of the West" lmao

62282767
>>62278448
token not needed

62282801
>>62280714
No they aren't.

62282929
>>62282801

62284091
>>62275698
>>62277437
buy signal

62284100
>>62281020
>crypto is banned in india
just googled it and you're full of shit

62284157
>>62277590
>I remember you could play Super Mario 64 on the Internet Computer Protocol (bing bing wahoo on ICP, jej) but who the fuck cares?
The whole point of that experiment killed ICP the moment it was taken off. What's the point of your web infrastructure if regular web rules still apply to it? Why the fuck is your product worth dick if you're just reinventing the wheel?
>buh muh token costs!!
Irrelevant. If price to run the grid increases because token become more valuable, then whole economic incentive to migrate to your web infrastructure falls apart.

62284265
>>62275591
did they ever get dapps?

62284324
>>62284091
>buy signal

under $20k btc

62284332
>>62284265
i don't think so, who wants to learn haskell

62287225
lol, lmao, etc

62287288
>still worth more than xlm
Tragic

62290899
>>62287225
Just lmfao

62290974
>>62284332
Haskell is arguably one of the worst programming languages ever invented.

62291005
>>62287225
wth is rain
>>62275591
saw this happening when they didn't integrate with Chainlink

62291011
>>62290974
plutus core isn't strictly speaking haskell
it's lambda- calculus
but that's not the reason cardano struggles to have a virtual machine

anyway, cardano can't really "collapse" because its TVL is already very low, there isn't really any money moving, so the security budget of its staking BFT is enough to order everything correctly
but I doubt it will significantly have any real volume either

62291214
https://x.com/IOHK_Charles/status/2060082349963120728

62292080
>>62277437
>t. uninformed retard
Blockchain and DLT actually solve real problems even in regulated markets. You think JPM, Goldman, and the rest of the boomer institutions would dump resources into this just for fun? Use your brain.
It solves a mountain of boring (but expensive) problems that tech-illiterate normies don't even know exist: automated financial workflows, instant settlement, reconciliation, data integrity, and compliance. All baked into a single tamper-proof tech stack.
If you can't see the value in that, just stay quiet and keep posting your 50x memecoin gambles instead of pretending to understand enterprise tech.

62292098
this is all you need to know >>62276491

62292107
>i ready a blog post that told me that big institutions will be buying the token
>i bought the token
>it's been 9 years and nobody is buying the token
>i wasn't scammed though, they will buy the token very soon you guys

62292821
>>62291214
Pure cope

One scammer trying to feel superior to another scammer

62292914
>>62277151
> starts up a clinic
> starts up construction firms to build the clinic
If only people knew how much money some people have. It's like how streamers all go through a stage where they have loads of gear and fancy backgrounds then learn that impacts donations so suddenly revert to plain bedrooms. Don't want the masses to know you're pulling in $1k an hour.

62292922
>>62277590
what is the point of making a web3 infrastructure that can play a game that will run via emulator on literally the cheapest, nastiest laptops money can buy right now?

62292925
>>62292080
>instant settlement
way to phrase a problem as a solution LMAO, you must be jewish with this level of pilpul kek.

62293011
>>62292821
you may not like charles methods but he has a lot of money to give to Chainlink labs

item.title: ITT: Post an image that sums up /biz/ succinctly — 42 replies — 6.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
ITT: Post an image that sums up /biz/ succinctly

Open thread • Replies: 42 • Updated: 2026-05-30 22:26 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62285243
>>62285128
crazy the amount of time people spend fudding a random alt coin.
it happens so much, the amount of energy that's spent on it buy a group of individuals, or a bot? either way, it's very interesting from the outside
made me start to think the linkies are onto something..
to anyone else on the outside, like anyone here from pmg or smg? how does this look? pretty crazy to me..
anyway the constant fud against link made me think i should secure a suicide bag of it, and now i just buy 1 link everytime someone posts a fud thread for free, in their own spare time.. which is crazy to me
so must be trying to hide something

62285245
quick, make more threads!

62285257
>>62285128
so true, king

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62287615
>>62287261

>I'm DCAing something I don't even understand that is dieing because it "owns the transgenic"

lmao what a fucking retard

62287648
>>62287615
retards retard
retards win

62287707
>>62285243
nah it's just a natural reaction to the link shill spam that's been going on since 2017, but you know this since you are a shill

62287709
>>62287615
>dieing
I haven't owned Link since 2020 but I might just buy some after reading your post

62287712
Very effective link thread. I'm even tempted to buy it, even though I don't do crypto.

62287714
>>62285535
What is a percentage

62287716
>>62287712
>"Hello fellow huwhite saars, by vishnu i think we should all redeem our apple gift cards and buy link tokens saars, berry good coin saar"

62287729
>>62287261
Both of them keep it to a general. Linkfaggots, in contrast, spammed /biz/ for years.

62287741
>>62285243
you're a mentally retarded cuckold LARPing as a non LINK holder. Chainlink is trading at a lower price than 6 years ago

62288220
>>62287729
>>62287707
>spam up the board as a response to link spam
genuis
>>62287741
i have a little link because of the constant schizo war between fudders and shills, just makes me think i should have a little in case
I never even told anyone to buy link in this thread, link isn't even 1 percent of my port.

62288375
>>62285882
underrated (i don't even use this board but it sums up 4chan pretty nicely)

62288389
>>62285128
>AI slop
Yep. This is /biz/ alright.

62288421
>>62285128

62288446
>>62285243

wait so you do individual $9 buys of an altcoin to "own" random dudes online?

how are the fees on those buys champ

grim

62288528
>>62288365
This is me but i somehow ended up with a career, a wife and a house.

62288547
>>62288446
no fees bro, not that hard to figure out. I'm not using market order hot swaps or some shit. I have fee free trading on a CEX

62288565
>>62285128

62291294
>>62285882
sucks to your austhism

62291316
>>62288565
Are they friends?

62293121
>>62285243
See all the seething replies to your post, should tell you something

62293333
>>62285128

62293357
>>62288365
>>62288528
So it isn’t you at all.

62293398
>>62285128

62293403
>>62285243
It is weird. If a normal person finds a coin or stock garbage, they just ignore it instead of wasting any time it. Incessantly spamming against it is very, very odd.

62293409
>>62293398
nice thumbnail faggot

62293483
>>62293333
>checked
i remember rlc got shilled hard by this

62293490
>>62288365
>barely 4000 hours on steam at 30

come on now, thats normie tier
